steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Standard English Conventions question testing punctuation of a parenthetical aside. The phrase “according to Swentzell” interrupts the sentence between the subject (“The arrangement of the figures”) and the verb (“represents”). A comma already appears before “according,” so the blank must supply the closing punctuation that matches it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Swentzell: No punctuation after the aside leaves the opening comma unmatched, violating the rule that a parenthetical interruption must be enclosed by a pair of matching punctuation marks. ✗
  • Choice B — Swentzell,: A closing comma matches the opening comma, correctly enclosing the aside “according to Swentzell” and cleanly separating it from the verb. ✓
  • Choice C — Swentzell:: A colon signals that an independent clause precedes it, but “The arrangement of the figures, according to Swentzell” is not an independent clause, so a colon is grammatically incorrect here. ✗
  • Choice D — Swentzell—: A dash does not match the comma that opened the aside; paired punctuation must be the same type on both sides (comma–comma or dash–dash). ✗
That is the correct answer.
